Node started: Derive the partition function Z(x, n, l, T) for a freely jointed chain of n segments of length l extended to a total end-to-end distance x. Express the Helmholtz free energy A(x, T) and the entropy S(x, T) as functions of x, n, l, and T. Use the high-temperature/small-extension approximation where applicable.

Node started: Given the entropy S(x, T) derived from the statistical mechanics step, apply the adiabatic condition (dS = 0) to find the relationship between temperature T and extension x. Then, use the thermodynamic identity for force under adiabatic conditions, F = (dE/dx)_S or F = T(dS/dx)_E, to derive the force law F(x).
